messages.content: Build a pure Decider for a Ticket aggregate in TypeScript. Export initialState, decide, and evolve as a Decider<Command, Event, State>. Throw a typed error when closing an already-closed ticket.
Write a Decider for a Project aggregate covering plan, start, complete, and archive transitions. Starting a completed project must throw. Strict types, exhaustive never default.
Explain briefly how a Decider handles a Sprint aggregate, then implement one with begin, addStoryPoints, and close transitions in TypeScript. Strict types.
Implement a Task aggregate as a Decider in TypeScript. Constraints: strict types, throw on reassignment of a completed task, exhaustive never default.
Write a Decider for a Milestone aggregate. Commands: defineMilestone, markReached, postpone. Postponing a reached milestone must throw. TypeScript.
Build a Decider for an Epic aggregate. The epic can be split into child tasks; commands are create, addChildTask, and finalize. Finalizing with zero children must throw.
Write a projection that builds a WorkOrder status view in TypeScript. Use project(view, event), and ignore events the view does not care about by returning it unchanged.
Implement a Ledger balance projection that tracks running balances per account. Exhaustive switch on event.type, strict types, TypeScript.
Show a Backlog projection that counts tickets by status. Ignore events that do not change status counts. TypeScript, strict types.
Build an Allocation projection that tracks hours allocated per assignee across tasks. Return the view unchanged for events outside the view's interest.
Implement a versioned Snapshot loader for a Journal aggregate. CURRENT_VERSION is 3 with v1-to-v2 and v2-to-v3 upgraders. TypeScript, strict types.
How do I add a Snapshot for a Sprint aggregate with a versioned upgrade pipeline in TypeScript? Show a minimal working example with two upgraders.
Add versioned Snapshot upgrading for an Approval aggregate. CURRENT_VERSION is 4 with three upgraders keyed by source version. Use a per-version map.
Implement an optimistic-concurrency appendToStream wrapper for a Ticket aggregate. Surface a typed ConcurrencyConflict on revision mismatch. Local EventStore interface.
Show the v5-style optimistic concurrency pattern for appending Project events. Read the current revision, append with expectedRevision, translate the store's conflict into a typed error.
Refactor this Task append flow to use expectedRevision with a typed ConcurrencyConflict. Add a retry-once helper that reloads and retries on conflict.
Write a CommandHandler for a Ticket aggregate over an EventStore. The handler loads, decides, and appends with expectedRevision. Local EventStore interface.
Build a generic CommandHandler factory parameterized by a Decider and an EventStore. It returns an async handle(streamName, command) that loads, decides, and appends. TypeScript.
Build a CommandHandler for a WorkOrder aggregate that wraps the IO boundary in Effect.gen. Load, decide, append with expectedRevision.
Explain briefly how a CommandHandler differs from a Decider, then implement a handler for an Entry aggregate in a Journal using Effect.gen for IO.
Build a subscription runner that resumes a WorkOrder projection from a persisted checkpoint. Crash-safe: apply, persist, then write checkpoint. Local CheckpointStore interface.
Show the v5 way to run a checkpointed projection subscription using Effect.gen for the IO boundary. Minimal example.
Add resumable checkpointing to this chunked polling subscription. The CheckpointStore must expose read and write; writes happen after a successful projection apply.
Show the idiomatic generic aggregateStream<S, E> helper for folding an AsyncIterable of events into state. TypeScript, strict types, no mutation of input.
Write Oskar-style when + aggregateStream for a Task aggregate with a typed check guard in TypeScript. Each event should carry a data property.
Implement an aggregate reducer and when function for a Ledger account. Provide an exhaustive switch with never default and a helper aggregate(events) returning state.
Implement evolve as a pure reducer over an Epic event stream, returning the aggregate state. Discriminated events, exhaustive switch, TypeScript.
Write a Project aggregate using when() with a partial state reducer and a type guard isProject. Include an aggregateStream helper that enforces the guard.
Show how to upcast old Ticket events to the current shape. The old TicketOpened v1 has reporter: string; the current v2 has reporter: { id, displayName }.
Write an upcasting function for Sprint events. v1 has goal: string; v2 renames it to objective and adds a startedAt. Use a raw-event DU and throw on unknown.
Implement a resumable read-model rebuild that wipes the projection store and re-projects WorkOrder events from revision 0 in batches. Reports progress.
Write a chunked read-model rebuild for a Journal view. It resumes from a cursor and calls project per event. Include a wipe-first flag.
Build a minimal CQRS boundary for a Ticket aggregate. handleCommand uses a Decider and an EventStore; queryView reads from a denormalised read store. No shared state type.
Write a CQRS example for a Project aggregate. Write side goes through a Decider + EventStore; read side exposes a denormalised ProjectStatusView. Eventually consistent.
Write a stateless process manager that reacts to TaskCompleted by emitting RequestApproval, and to ApprovalGranted by emitting MarkTaskVerified. Pure react function, TypeScript.
Build a stateful process manager for a Project-to-Milestone saga. Encode the state machine as (state, event) => { nextState, command? }. TypeScript.
Implement an integration-event translation for a Journal domain. Map EntryPosted and JournalClosed to versioned IntegrationEvents; internal events translate to null.
Build an outbox-append helper for a Ticket aggregate. Atomically append domain events and integration rows within one transaction. A publisher worker polls outbox rows separately.
Fix this non-exhaustive evolve for a Ledger entry. It must cover every event with a never default and return the current state for no-op events.
Build a Decider plus CommandHandler for a Backlog Entry aggregate. Wrap the IO boundary in Effect.gen and use expectedRevision on append.
